name: Ormellius Goldwine
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears a black and burgundy outfit with gold-trimmed shoes - expensive upper-class attire befitting his station as Count. Wore a piece of jewelry and carried the Colovian Signet Ring, the symbol of his authority.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: noble, dutiful, tragic, fallen
Role: Ormellius Goldwine was the Count of Kvatch, ruler of the city and guardian of its people. He held the Colovian Signet Ring, the symbol of his authority and position.
Home Location Information: Castle Kvatch, specifically the Count's Quarters in the back of the castle, where he met his end.
The Fall of Kvatch: When the Daedric invasion of Kvatch began, Count Goldwine was in his castle. Despite the chaos erupting around him and the siege that descended upon his city, he never made it to safety. He fell in the Count's Quarters, lying face-down in a pool of blood in the western corner of the room. His death came before anyone could reach him, and the Colovian Signet Ring remained with his body - a symbol of authority that would need to be passed to a new Count when Kvatch could crown one.
Captain Savlian's Hope: Savlian Matius, Kvatch's Captain of the Guard, held out hope that the Count might still be alive even after the siege was broken. He sent others to search the castle's interior, desperate to find his lord before it was too late. The discovery of the Count's death was a devastating blow to Savlian, who lamented that they had not arrived sooner.
Personality - The Fallen Lord: Count Goldwine died defending his city or attempting to escape the sudden Daedric onslaught. His body in the Count's Quarters tells a story of a leader who remained in his castle as chaos descended, whether by choice or circumstance. Even in death, he commanded respect - Savlian Matius reacted with fury when anyone spoke of him mockingly, showing the loyalty and devotion the Count inspired in his guards. His legacy would be a city in ruins and people scattered, but also a testament to Kvatch's resilience - those who survived would fight to reclaim what their Count had lost.
author: Bethesda
